content.full_text:
Law Offices of Jerrold M. Ladar
ATTORNEY AT LAW
SUITE 310 507 POLK ST.
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94102-3384
(415) 928-2333

June 12, 1990

Attn: Margaret Ellison
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Kevin Starr, Treasurer, No on P, Yes on V Committee,
ID Number: 892006

Dear Ms. Ellison,

I am writing this letter to request an opinion whether a recipient committee may pay the fees and costs incurred for consulting an attorney and retaining the attorney to act on behalf of the committee during an investigation by the local District Attorney and the F.P.P.C. and to bring the committee into compliance with filing requirements.

"No on P, Yes on V" recipient committee was formed to oppose and support local propositions in the November 7, 1989 election in San Francisco. On the eve of the election, Mayor Art Agnos publicly accused the No on P, Yes on V Committee and Kevin Starr, Treasurer, of failing to comply with reporting requirements in order to defraud the San Francisco electorate by hiding the fact that Sacramento money was funding an effort to defeat the new San Francisco baseball park. This launched an investigation by both the F.P.P.C. and the San Francisco District Attorney's Office to determine whether there had been any wrongdoing in connection with the No on P, Yes on V Committee, the reporting and funding.

Kevin Starr retained our law firm on behalf of the Committee. We drafted required forms, prepared and filed them. We counselled the officers throughout the investigations and grand jury proceedings. At all times the officers cooperated with the investigations and grand jury. Although four political consultants were subsequently charged with misdemeanor violations by the San Francisco District Attorney, no charges were filed against the officers individually or by virtue of their position on the Committee.

Fair Political Practices Commission  
June 12, 1990  
Page 2

I am attaching a copy of the billing by my firm in this matter. I have removed certain names to preserve confidentiality. I have identified other names to make the bill more comprehensible. Please advise us if paying this bill is a permissible Committee expenditure.

We are ready to terminate the committee but await your opinion.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
JOYCE B. LADAR

JBL.mb  
3292.FPPC  
Encl.

cc: Client
